Wetland Cover Change

tinytex::tlmgr_install(c("fvextra", "xcolor", "framed"))

library

library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)

import data

Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR <- read.csv("~/Documents/Wetland Research Program/Wetland River Rapport Indicator/Wetland Cover Change LRR.csv")

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

knitr::kable(Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "scale_down")

name data

data=Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR

#Local - Coastal Wetland

Plot out the change detection studies that looked at individual wetlands

filter out local data

local_data <- data %>%
  filter(Scale == "Local- Coastal Wetland")

Set arrow position just outside plot (left side)

arrow_x <- min(local_data$mean.year) -25
arrow_height <- 0.4

alternative palette

my_cols <- c(
  "#1B9E77", # teal
  "#D95F02", # orange
  "#7570B3", # purple
  "#E7298A", # pink
  "#66A61E", # green
  "#E6AB02", # mustard
  "#A6761D", # brown
  "#666666", # gray
  "#1F78B4", # blue
  "#B2DF8A", # light green
  "#FB9A99", # light red
  "#CAB2D6"  # lavender
)

plot no arrows

a=ggplot(local_data, aes(x = mean.year, y = LRR..ln.A..A.., color = Site)) +
  
  # Study duration lines
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = start.date,
      xend = end.date,
      y = LRR..ln.A..A..,
      yend = LRR..ln.A..A..
    ),
     linewidth = 1.2,alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  
  # Zero reference line
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols)+
  

  # Axis + title labels
  labs(
    x = "Time Period",
    y = "LRR (Overall Wetland Change)",
    title = " ",
    color = "Wetland Site"
  ) +  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1950, xend = 1950,
    y = 0.55, yend = 0.65,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1950,
    y = 0.68,
    label = "Gain",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) +

  # ----------------------------
  # Loss arrow (BOTTOM, outside plot)
  # ----------------------------
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1950, xend = 1950,
    y = -0.63, yend = -0.73,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1950,
    y = -0.75,
    label = "Loss",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) +

  # Allow drawing outside plot area
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +

  # Add extra space above + below
 theme_minimal() +theme(
  plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
)+ theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.2)
  )+theme(legend.position = "right")+theme(
panel.grid = element_blank()
)+theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))+theme(
  axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 11))+theme(
  legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "cm"),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 12))
a

Regional

Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR_noNA <- read.csv("~/Documents/Wetland Research Program/Wetland River Rapport Indicator/Wetland Cover Change LRRnoNA.csv")

name data

data=Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR_noNA

Plot out the change detection studies that looked at regional (river sections) coastal areas

filter out local data

regional_data <- data %>%
  filter(Scale == "Regional - Coastal Wetlands")

Set arrow position just outside plot (left side)

arrow_x <- min(regional_data$mean.year) -25
arrow_height <- 0.4
my_cols <- c(
  "#1B9E77", # teal
  "#D95F02", # orange
  "#E7298A", # pink
  "#66A61E", # green
  "#E6AB02", # mustard
  "#A6761D", # brown
  "#666666", # gray
  "#1F78B4", # blue
  "#B2DF8A", # light green
  "#FB9A99", # light red
  "#CAB2D6"  # lavender
)

reorder

regional_data$River.Section=factor(regional_data$River.Section,levels=c("USLR",  "Thousand Islands and Brockville",
  "Brockville",    "Lake St. Lawrence and Akwesasne/Cornwall","Akwesasne/Cornwall and Lac Saint-François",
  "Lac Saint-François",
   "LSLR",
  "Lac Saint-Louis", 
  "Montreal East","Montreal East/Contrecoeur",
  "Contrecoeur"))
regional_data$Project=factor(regional_data$Project,levels=c("USLR", "KEPO"))

plot no arrows



r=ggplot(regional_data, aes(x = mean.year, y = LRR..ln.A..A.., color = River.Section)) +
  
  # Study duration lines
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = start.date,
      xend = end.date,
      y = LRR..ln.A..A..,
      yend = LRR..ln.A..A..
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  
  # Zero reference line
 geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
           linetype = "dashed",
           linewidth = 0.5,
           color = "gray40")+ scale_color_manual(values = my_cols)+
  

  # Axis + title labels
  labs(
    x = "Time Period",
    y = "LRR (Overall Wetland Change)",
    title = " ",
    color = "River Section"
  ) +  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1940, xend = 1940,
    y = 1.45, yend = 1.55,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1940,
    y = 1.6,
    label = "Gain",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) +

  # ----------------------------
  # Loss arrow (BOTTOM, outside plot)
  # ----------------------------
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1940, xend = 1940,
    y = -0.83, yend = -0.93,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1940,
    y = -1,
    label = "Loss",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) + facet_wrap(~Panel)+

  # Allow drawing outside plot area
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +

  # Add extra space above + below
  theme_minimal() +theme(
  plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
)+ theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.2)
  )+theme(legend.position = "bottom")+theme(
panel.grid = element_blank()
)+theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))+theme(
  axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 11))+theme(
  legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "cm"),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 9))
r

#watershed

Plot out the change detection studies that looked at regional (river sections) coastal areas

filter out local data

watershed_data <- data %>%
  filter(Scale == "Watershed")

Set arrow position just outside plot (left side)

arrow_x <- min(watershed_data$mean.year) -25
arrow_height <- 0.4

set colours

my_cols<- c(
  "#1B9E77", # teal
  "#D95F02", # orange
  "#7570B3", # purple
  "#E7298A", # pink
  "#66A61E", # green
  "#E6AB02", # mustard
  "#A6761D", # brown
  "#666666", # gray
  "#1F78B4", # blue
  "#B2DF8A", # light green
  "#FB9A99", # light red
  "#CAB2D6", # lavender
  "#17BECF"  # cyan (NEW)
)

reorder

watershed_data$River.Section=factor(watershed_data$River.Section,levels=c("Leeds Township",
"Grenville Township",
"Dundas Township",
"Stormont Township",
"Glengary Township", "Indian subwatershed","Oswegatchi subwatershed",
"Grass subwatershed",
"Raquette subwatershed",
"Saint Regis subwatershed",
"Salmon subwatershed",
"LSLR",
"Kahnawake Region of Interest"
))
w=ggplot(watershed_data, aes(x = mean.year, y = LRR..ln.A..A.., color = River.Section)) +
  
  # Study duration lines
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = start.date,
      xend = end.date,
      y = LRR..ln.A..A..,
      yend = LRR..ln.A..A..
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  
  # Zero reference line
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols)+
  

  # Axis + title labels
  labs(
    x = "Time Period",
    y = "LRR (Overall Wetland Change)",
    title = " ",
    color = "Portions of Watersheds"
  ) +  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1925, xend = 1925,
    y = 0.05, yend = 0.06,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1925,
    y = 0.065,
    label = "Gain",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) +

  # ----------------------------
  # Loss arrow (BOTTOM, outside plot)
  # ----------------------------
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x = 1925, xend = 1925,
    y = -0.125, yend = -0.135,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")),
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1925,
    y = -0.14,
    label = "Loss",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2
  ) +

  # Allow drawing outside plot area
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") + facet_wrap(~Panel)+

  # Add extra space above + below
  theme_minimal() +theme(
  plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
)+ theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.2)
  )+theme(legend.position = "bottom")+theme(
panel.grid = element_blank()
)+theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))+theme(
  axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 11))+theme(
  legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "cm"),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 9))
  
w

#Wetland Change Comparison of Different Scales

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)


# Read the CSV properly
wetland_df <- Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR_noNA



# Desired order (TOP → BOTTOM in plot)
scale_order <- c("Local- Coastal Wetland",
                 "Regional - Coastal Wetlands",
                 "Watershed")

filtered_df <- wetland_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(Scale %in% scale_order) %>%
  mutate(
    Scale = factor(Scale, levels = rev(scale_order)),
    Project = factor(Project, levels = c("USLR", "KEPO"))
  )



# Filter + reorder factor
filtered_df <- wetland_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(Scale %in% scale_order) %>%
  mutate(
    Scale = factor(Scale, levels = rev(scale_order))  # <-- reverse fixes ordering
  )

summary_df <- filtered_df %>%
  group_by(Scale, Project) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(Annual.rate)),
    mean_rate = mean(Annual.rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rate   = sd(Annual.rate, na.rm = TRUE),

    se_rate   = sd_rate / sqrt(n),

    ci_lower  = mean_rate - 1.96 * se_rate,
    ci_upper  = mean_rate + 1.96 * se_rate,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

x = ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = mean_rate, y = Scale, color = Project)) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper),
    height = 0.25,
    linewidth = 1,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
  ) +

  geom_point(
    size = 4,
    shape = 18,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
   "USLR" = "#1f78b4",
"KEPO" = "#33a02c"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(
    "Local- Coastal Wetland"      = "Local",
    "Regional - Coastal Wetlands" = "Regional",
    "Watershed"                   = "Watershed"
  )) +

  labs(
    x = "Annual Rate of Wetland Change (Mean LRR ± 95% CI)",
    y = "",
    color = "Project"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 14)
x

R code anova

# Load libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load the dataset
# -----------------------------
wetland_df <- Wetland.Cover.Change.LRR

# -----------------------------
# 2. Keep only the 3 scale groups
# -----------------------------
scale_groups <- c("Local- Coastal Wetland",
                  "Regional - Coastal Wetlands",
                  "Watershed")

filtered_df <- wetland_df %>%
  filter(Scale %in% scale_groups)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Rename scale labels
# -----------------------------
filtered_df <- filtered_df %>%
  mutate(
    Scale_clean = case_when(
      Scale == "Local- Coastal Wetland" ~ "Local",
      Scale == "Regional - Coastal Wetlands" ~ "Regional",
      Scale == "Watershed" ~ "Watershed"
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 4. Remove missing values
# -----------------------------
filtered_df <- filtered_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Annual.rate),
         !is.na(Sections))

# -----------------------------
# 5. Loop through each scale
# -----------------------------
for (s in unique(filtered_df$Scale_clean)) {

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("Scale:", s, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  scale_data <- filtered_df %>%
    filter(Scale_clean == s)

  # -----------------------------
  # ANOVA test
  # -----------------------------
  cat("\n--- ANOVA Results ---\n")
  anova_model <- aov(Annual.rate ~ Sections, data = scale_data)
  print(summary(anova_model))

  # -----------------------------
  # Kruskal-Wallis test (backup)
  # -----------------------------
  cat("\n--- Kruskal-Wallis Results ---\n")
  print(kruskal.test(Annual.rate ~ Sections, data = scale_data))

}

====================================
Scale: Regional 
====================================

--- ANOVA Results ---
            Df   Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
Sections    13 0.028776 0.0022136   11.13 7.14e-06 ***
Residuals   17 0.003381 0.0001989                     
---
Signif. codes:  
0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

--- Kruskal-Wallis Results ---

    Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

data:  Annual.rate by Sections
Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 20.058, df = 13, p-value
= 0.09376


====================================
Scale: Local 
====================================

--- ANOVA Results ---
            Df    Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
Sections    11 0.0006833 6.212e-05   0.877  0.691
Residuals    1 0.0000708 7.080e-05               

--- Kruskal-Wallis Results ---

    Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

data:  Annual.rate by Sections
Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 10.385, df = 11, p-value
= 0.4962


====================================
Scale: Watershed 
====================================

--- ANOVA Results ---
            Df    Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
Sections    12 3.241e-05 2.701e-06    1.31  0.601
Residuals    1 2.060e-06 2.062e-06               

--- Kruskal-Wallis Results ---

    Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

data:  Annual.rate by Sections
Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 12.886, df = 12, p-value
= 0.3774
m=ggplot(filtered_df,
       aes(x = Sections, y = Annual.rate)) +

  geom_boxplot() +

  facet_wrap(~Scale_clean, scales = "free_x") +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "gray40") +

  labs(
    x = "Location",
    y = "Annual Rate of Wetland Cover Change"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
m
